content:
# Romans Chapter 14 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ROM_014_page_001.jpg

1. And the [one] being weak in the faith receive, not for judgments of reasonings.

2. One believes to eat all things, but the [one] being weak eats vegetables.

3. The [one] eating [is] not to despise the [one] not eating; and the [one] not eating [is] not to judge the [one] eating; for the God received him.

4. Who are you, the [one] judging another's household servant? To the own lord he stands or falls. And he will be made to stand, for the Lord is able to make him stand.

5. One judges [one] day above [another] day; another judges every day [alike]. Let each be fully convinced in the own mind.

6. The [one] regarding the day, regards [it] to [the] Lord; and the [one] eating, eats to [the] Lord, for he gives thanks to the God; and the [one] not eating, to [the] Lord does not eat, and gives thanks to the God.

7. For none of us lives to himself, and none dies to himself.

8. For if we live, to the Lord we live; and if we die, to the Lord we die. Therefore whether we live or whether we die, we are of the Lord.

9. For to this [end] Christos died and lived, that he might be Lord of both [the] dead and [the] living.

10. But you, why do you judge the brother of you? Or also you, why do you despise the brother of you? For we will all stand before the judgment seat of the God.

11. For it has been written, "As I live, says [the] Lord, that to me every knee will bow, and every tongue will confess to the God."

12. So then each of us will give account concerning himself to the God.

13. Therefore let us no longer judge one another; but rather judge this — not to place a stumbling block or an offense before the brother.

14. I know and am persuaded in [the] Lord Yeshua that nothing [is] common through itself; except to the [one] reckoning anything to be common, to that [one it is] common.

15. For if because of food the brother of you is grieved, you are no longer walking according to love. Do not by the food of you destroy that [one] for whom Christos died.

16. Therefore do not let the good of you be spoken of as evil.

17. For the kingdom of the God is not eating and drinking, but righteousness and peace and joy in [the] Holy Spirit.

18. For the [one] serving the Christos in these things [is] well-pleasing to the God and approved by humans.

19. So then let us pursue the [things] of the peace and the [things] of the building up of one another.

20. Do not for the sake of food tear down the work of the God. All things indeed [are] clean, but [it is] evil to the human eating through a stumbling block.

21. [It is] good not to eat meat nor to drink wine, nor [to do anything] by which the brother of you stumbles.

22. The faith which you have, have [it] according to yourself before the God. Blessed [is] the [one] not judging himself in what he approves.

23. But the [one] doubting, if he eats, has been condemned, because [it is] not from faith. And everything which [is] not from faith is sin.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.10 — "Judgment seat of God":** Sinaiticus reads τῷ βήματι τοῦ θεοῦ ("judgment seat of God"). Some manuscripts read "of Christ" (cf. 2 Corinthians 5:10). The Sinaiticus reading emphasizes God's role as final judge.

2. **v.11 — Isaiah 45:23 quotation:** In Isaiah, this refers to YHWH. Paul applies it to God's universal judgment. In Philippians 2:10-11, the same Isaiah text is applied to Christ. The interchangeability is theologically significant.

3. **v.14 — "Nothing is common through itself":** κοινόν (common/profane/unclean) — the opposite of holy. Paul declares all foods ritually clean, echoing Jesus' teaching (Mark 7:19). This is the end of Torah food laws as binding obligations.

4. **v.23 — "Everything not from faith is sin":** A maxim that extends beyond food disputes. The principle: if you cannot do something in good conscience before God, do not do it. Conscience is the arbiter of permissible behavior in disputable matters.

— EMET, AI Translator
